rationale: The prior attempt asserted the antecedent of the postcondition implication verbatim, but those forall facts are not in scope inside the body — they only become hypotheses when Verus checks the postcondition at exit, so the asserts failed. Instead, call `const_facts`, `self_.lemma_view`, and `reveal(is_bit_set)`, then re-state lemma_view's two ensures (unconditionally true here) so the `self_@.contains(t)` trigger surface is primed; also prove the range bound `self_@.contains(i) ==> 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS` so the two `forall |t|` quantifiers (r1 and r2 ranges) line up against the same bit-level facts when the implication is discharged at the postcondition check.
